bullets.headline: Fed paused at 3.50-3.75%; AGG returns ~6.3% trailing year
bullets.summary: The Fed has paused rate cuts with the funds rate at 3.50-3.75% since December 2025; 10-year Treasury yields near 4.3% have rekindled demand for investment-grade fixed income, and AGG returned ~6.3% over the trailing year. Morningstar awarded the fund its Gold medal as of April 30, 2026.

bullets.headline: Yields range-bound 3.75-4.25%; AGG income profile holds
bullets.summary: Market pricing implies only limited further Fed easing toward a 3.0-3.25% terminal rate amid sticky inflation near 2.5-3%, keeping long-term yields range-bound roughly 3.75-4.25% and capping sharp price rallies in AGG but supporting its income profile.

bullets.headline: BIL ~3.91% yield; near-zero-duration cash proxy
bullets.summary: BIL is yielding approximately 3.91% as of May 2026 and last went ex-dividend on May 1, 2026. The ETF holds only US Treasury bills with 1-3 month maturities, making it a near-zero-duration cash proxy.

bullets.headline: BIL carry attractive vs long-duration bonds
bullets.summary: With the Fed paused and yields range-bound, BIL's ~3.9% carry remains attractive relative to long-duration bonds on a risk-adjusted basis; no material fund-specific news flagged in the lookback window.

bullets.headline: Gold hit $5,595/oz Jan 29; IAU +15% YTD then pulled back
bullets.summary: Gold hit an all-time high of $5,595/oz on January 29, 2026, driven by safe-haven demand from tariff uncertainty, central bank buying, and ETF inflows. XAU/USD is up approximately 15% year-to-date as of early May, though IAU has pulled back ~5.4% from its Q1 peak to trade near $86.82 on May 3.

bullets.headline: WGC: Q1 2026 demand at record; JPM target $5,000
bullets.summary: World Gold Council reported global gold demand inched up in Q1 2026 to a record high; J.P. Morgan Global Research sees prices pushing toward $5,000/oz by Q4 2026, with $6,000/oz a longer-term scenario if recession risk rises.

bullets.headline: GOOGL Q1 2026: revenue $109.9B (+22%); Cloud +63% to $20B
bullets.summary: Alphabet reported Q1 2026 revenue of $109.9B (+22% YoY), net income of $62.6B (+81% YoY). Google Cloud surged 63% to $20B; enterprise AI solutions named the primary Cloud growth driver. Cloud backlog nearly doubled QoQ to over $460B. Full-year capex guidance raised to $180-190B.

bullets.headline: Google TPUv7 picks up Anthropic and Meta as external customers
bullets.summary: SemiAnalysis reports Google's TPUv7 is seeing massive upward order revisions in the supply chain for 2026; Anthropic and Meta confirmed as major external TPU customers, marking Google's first broad external sale of its AI infrastructure hardware.

bullets.headline: Google Willow chip on Milestone 2; Quantum Echoes shows verifiable advantage
bullets.summary: Google Quantum AI's Willow chip (105 qubits) is on Milestone 2 of Google's six-step roadmap; the company is accepting research proposals for early Willow access through May 15, 2026. A Quantum Echoes algorithm result published in early 2026 demonstrated verifiable quantum advantage for a practical simulation workload.

bullets.headline: NVDA Q1 FY26: revenue $39.1B +73%; Blackwell fastest ramp ever
bullets.summary: NVIDIA reported fiscal Q1 2026 revenue of $39.1B (+73% YoY, +10% QoQ), the fastest Blackwell ramp in company history. Q2 2026 guidance is $45B (+/-2%). The company absorbed a $4.5B inventory write-down on H20 chips tied to April 2025 export controls; CFO noted an additional ~$8B in H20 revenue will evaporate in Q2 as China access remains restricted.

bullets.headline: AMD MI450X production H2 2026; NVDA supply-chain advantage holds
bullets.summary: SemiAnalysis detailed AMD's competitive window: AMD's MI450X rack-scale solution could reach production in H2 2026 and approach parity with NVIDIA's VR200 NVL144. Most frontier labs and inference providers are still not running FP8 or single-node inference, sustaining NVIDIA's near-term supply-chain advantage.

bullets.headline: China H200 ban lifted; Nvidia volume still constrained, Huawei competitor rising
bullets.summary: President Trump lifted the AI chip ban on China in December 2025 allowing H200 shipments conditional on the US receiving a 25% cut of sales; however Nvidia has still not moved material H200 volume to China as of February 2026, and analysts warned that Chinese domestic alternatives (Huawei CloudMatrix) are eroding the addressable market.

bullets.headline: MSFT FY26 Q3: revenue $77.7B (+18%); Azure +40%; bookings +112%
bullets.summary: Microsoft reported FY26 Q3 (calendar Q1 2026) revenue of $77.7B (+18% YoY); Azure grew 40% YoY. Commercial bookings jumped 112%, driven by a large OpenAI commitment and Azure/M365 deals above $100M. Full-year capex guidance raised to ~$190B, approximately $55B above prior consensus.

bullets.headline: Microsoft Majorana 1 topological qubit chip; new Denmark quantum lab
bullets.summary: Microsoft's Majorana 1 chip (the first quantum processor powered by topological qubits) is in active research; the company launched its 2026 Quantum Pioneers Program (QuPP) in January 2026 targeting measurement-based topological computing. A new 511,000 sq ft Quantum Lab in Lyngby, Denmark is under construction.

bullets.headline: RKLB Q1 2026 guide $185-200M (+57%); $1.85B backlog
bullets.summary: Q1 2026 earnings scheduled for May 7, 2026. Revenue guidance is $185-200M (+57% YoY). Backlog stands at $1.85B (+73% YoY) and cash/equivalents exceed $1B.

bullets.headline: Neutron debut delayed to Q4 2026 after tank-rupture root-cause fix
bullets.summary: Neutron's first launch is now targeted for Q4 2026 after a Stage 1 propellant tank ruptured during qualification testing (January 2026); root cause was a manufacturing defect in a hand-laid autoclave joint, now corrected with automated fiber placement. Launch permit paperwork filed for a July-December 2026 window.

bullets.headline: RKLB closes $155M Mynaric deal; adds laser comms and EU footprint
bullets.summary: Rocket Lab completed the $155.3M acquisition of Mynaric AG on April 14, 2026, adding laser optical communications terminals and establishing its first European footprint (Munich). Mynaric's CONDOR Mk3 terminals are part of Rocket Lab's $1.3B SDA Proliferated Warfighter Space Architecture prime contracts.

bullets.headline: Pentagon visits RKLB; 25+ Electron launches targeted for 2026
bullets.summary: Pentagon Secretary visited Rocket Lab for an 'Arsenal of Freedom' tour in Q1 2026; RKLB confirmed plans to bid on DoD medium-launch contracts with Neutron. The company set a 2026 Electron launch target of 25+ launches (~20% above 2025's record of 21).

bullets.headline: BOTZ +21.9% TTM; NVIDIA 8.2% weight makes it partly an AI-chip proxy
bullets.summary: BOTZ returned ~21.9% over the trailing year, managing $3.4B in AUM. Top holdings include ABB (8.9%), Keyence (8.8%), Fanuc (8.3%), NVIDIA (8.2%), and Intuitive Surgical (6.9%). NVIDIA's 8%+ weight means BOTZ behaves partly as an AI-chip proxy, not only a pure robotics play.

bullets.headline: Tesla Optimus production starts Q2 2026; humanoid market $108B
bullets.summary: Tesla announced Optimus production begins at Fremont in Q2 2026 targeting 1M units/year, with a $25B capex plan for AI/robotics. Precedence Research values the global technology robotics market at $108B in 2026, projecting 283% growth by 2035 to $416B.

bullets.headline: ARK rotates aggressively into gene editing for 2026; ARKG AUM ~$984M
bullets.summary: ARK Invest declared 2026 the 'definitive coming-out party for the genomic revolution' and executed a major portfolio rotation, trimming Tesla and RKLB to aggressively accumulate gene-editing names. Top ARKG holdings: CRISPR Therapeutics (9.7%), Twist Bioscience (9.7%), Tempus AI (9.6%), 10x Genomics (6.6%), Beam Therapeutics (5.5%). AUM ~$984M.

bullets.headline: Casgevy CRISPR rollout hits reimbursement and delivery bottleneck
bullets.summary: STAT News reported (February 2026) that Vertex's CRISPR treatment Casgevy (for sickle cell disease) hit an unexpected rollout bottleneck despite its commercial approval, flagging reimbursement and delivery complexity as near-term headwinds for gene-editing therapies.

bullets.headline: ARCH $3B life-sciences fund; genome editing market path $5B to $13B
bullets.summary: ARCH Venture Partners raised $3B for a life sciences fund focused on mental health, cell therapy, and gene editing; the global genome editing market is projected to grow from ~$5B in 2026 to >$13B by 2035. ARKG is up ~7.9% in the week ending April 14, 2026.

bullets.headline: QTUM crosses $4B AUM; 5-star Morningstar; +44.88% 1y; rotated to hardware
bullets.summary: Defiance QTUM ETF surpassed $4B in AUM as of March 31, 2026, earning a 5-star Morningstar rating. One-year return: 44.88% at NAV. Three-year annualized: 33.18%. On March 20, 2026 the fund executed a major strategic rebalance, shifting away from software-heavy exposure toward specialized quantum hardware providers.

bullets.headline: IBM $20B+ Chicago quantum hub; fault-tolerant target 2029
bullets.summary: IBM announced (April 29, 2026) a $20B+ economic-impact quantum hub in Chicago (750 jobs, Illinois Quantum and Microelectronics Park) and an evolved MIT-IBM Computing Research Lab targeting fault-tolerant quantum computing by 2029.

bullets.headline: Quantinuum IPO (Honeywell spinout) is a 2026 catalyst for QTUM
bullets.summary: Honeywell's planned IPO of Quantinuum (flagged in January 2026 filings) is a near-term catalyst that could reshape QTUM's composition and draw new institutional attention to the quantum sector.

bullets.headline: NUKZ $771M AUM; ~80% fission/uranium with SMR exposure; pure fusion pre-IPO
bullets.summary: NUKZ trades at ~$73.34 on May 4, 2026, with $771M in AUM. Top holdings: Cameco (9.9%), GE Vernova (3.7%), Talen Energy (3.1%), Constellation Energy (2.8%). The fund is ~80% fission-era companies (utilities, uranium, construction) with SMR developer exposure via Oklo, NuScale, and Nano Nuclear Energy. Pure-play fusion is not yet publicly investable at scale.

bullets.headline: Meta unlocks 6.6 GW nuclear for AI; 31.2 GW total announced capacity
bullets.summary: Meta announced nuclear energy projects in January 2026 (agreements with Vistra, TerraPower, Oklo, Constellation) unlocking up to 6.6 GW for AI data centers. 18 nuclear-powered AI data center facilities with 31.2 GW of announced capacity are tracked in 2026.

bullets.headline: FIA 2026 conf + IEA report; first demo plant target 2030
bullets.summary: FIA's 2026 Annual Policy Conference (March 18-19, Washington DC, 400+ leaders) and the IEA's State of Energy Innovation 2026 report (February 17) both elevated fusion's profile; the IEA's key benchmark is a first demonstration plant producing saleable energy by 2030.

bullets.headline: Oklo gets DoE Aurora SDA approvals; X-energy IPO adds SMR exposure
bullets.summary: Oklo received DoE approval for Nuclear Safety Design Agreements for its Aurora powerhouse (Idaho National Lab) and a second Texas site; it upsized its reactor design to 75 MWe to meet AI data-center demand. X-energy's recent IPO gave the NUKZ index its first pure-play SMR developer exposure.

AI.rationale: Revenue is real and compounding across all three AI tickers: GOOGL Cloud +63% to $20B; MSFT Azure +40% with 112% bookings growth; NVDA data-center +73% YoY. Capex is accelerating but absorbed by enterprise demand, not speculative overhang. Wave is past buildup, in clear adoption-compounding stage.

rockets_spacecraft.rationale: RKLB executing on Electron cadence and $1.85B backlog, but Neutron tank rupture pushes medium-launch revenue 6-9 months. Real commercial traction with limited market recognition outside dedicated space funds.

robotics.rationale: Tesla Optimus production start at Fremont Q2 2026, Figure AI's $700M raise, BOTZ's 21-37% TTM return. Real product launches and capital, not yet at unanimous-sell-side peak.

engineered_biology.rationale: ARKG AUM ~$984M, fund still ~80% below 2021 peak. Gene-editing therapies clearing clinical milestones (CRISPR, Beam, Casgevy) but commercial scaling early-stage. ARK aggressive rotation into gene editing, but market not yet pricing the inflection. Early, under-owned, cheap relative to trajectory.

quantum.rationale: QTUM hit $4B AUM with 44.88% 1y return and 5-star Morningstar rating; IBM expanded with $20B+ Chicago hub; Google Willow and MSFT Majorana 1 progressing; Quantinuum IPO imminent. Crossed from buildup into early surge: adoption compounding, but commercialization still largely pre-revenue.

nuclear_fusion.rationale: IEA 2026 report and FIA Annual Policy Conference signal growing institutional credibility. Corporate nuclear PPAs (Meta 6.6 GW) drive fission/SMR revenue today, but pure-play fusion still pre-IPO. IEA 2030 first-demonstration target means commercial fusion is 4+ years from revenue. NUKZ benefits from AI/data-center demand narrative, but pure fusion thesis is firmly buildup.

general_markets.rationale: AGG, BIL, IAU are macro instruments with no wave attachment. Fed pause, range-bound yields, gold at safe-haven highs.

thematic_concerns.concern: China export controls create a structural revenue gap: $4.5B Q1 inventory charge and ~$8B in lost Q2 H20 revenue. Even with the partial H200 reversal, Huawei CloudMatrix and domestic Chinese alternatives are filling the void.
thematic_concerns.evidence: CNBC (2026-02-26); SemiAnalysis on AMD MI450X and Huawei CloudMatrix.

thematic_concerns.concern: BOTZ's largest single position is NVIDIA (~8.2%), so the fund carries meaningful AI-chip concentration risk and overlaps with existing NVDA exposure.

Marketed as a 'nuclear renaissance' ETF but is predominantly a fission and uranium-infrastructure vehicle. Pure-play fusion firms not yet publicly traded; investors expecting fusion exposure will find conventional nuclear and SMR-development names.
thematic_concerns.evidence: Range ETFs holdings (2026-05-04): Cameco, GE Vernova, Talen, Constellation. FIA conference (2026-03-18): IEA 2030 first-demo benchmark.

thematic_concerns.concern: Small-cap gene-editing holdings (Twist, 10x Genomics, Beam) are particularly rate-sensitive. Casgevy rollout bottleneck illustrates clinical success does not equal commercial revenue on schedule.
thematic_concerns.evidence: STAT News (2026-02-05) Casgevy bottleneck; Yahoo Finance (2026-04-30) Fed pause at 3.50-3.75%.

watchlist_suggestions.rationale: RKLB is the only rockets/spacecraft name in the portfolio. Adding a second pure-play or broader space ETF would diversify within the wave and reduce single-stock Neutron-delay risk.

candidates.thesis_fit: Direct-to-cell satellite broadband; broadens space exposure beyond launch.
Diversified space-economy ETF (launch, satellite services, ground stations).

candidates.issuer_name: SPDR S&P Kensho Final Frontiers ETF
candidates.thesis_fit: Space and underwater exploration index; multi-name diversification.

watchlist_suggestions.rationale: ARKG is concentrated and actively managed (top 5 = ~41%). A complementary position in a broader biotech index or ILMN specifically would reduce ARK manager-concentration risk.

candidates.thesis_fit: Sequencing platform leader; infrastructure layer ARKG underweights.
Equal-weighted biotech index reduces single-fund manager risk.

watchlist_suggestions.rationale: BOTZ is solid but skews to Japanese industrials and overlaps with NVDA. ROBO is more equal-weighted and reduces the AI-chip concentration risk.

candidates.issuer_name: Robo Global Robotics and Automation Index ETF
candidates.thesis_fit: Equal-weighted robotics/automation; reduces NVDA overlap concentration.
